evidence.network: GET /health -> 200, body {"status":"ok"}, Content-Length: 16, X-Request-Id header present

steps.observed: Logs show: 'configuration loaded' (port 3199), 'database connection pool initialized', 'server starting' (addr :3199)

steps.expected: Graceful shutdown logs, process exits cleanly
steps.observed: Logs: 'server shutting down gracefully', 'server stopped cleanly', 'shutdown complete'. Process exited.

steps.action: Read /tmp/mreviewer-api.log and check each line is valid JSON
steps.expected: All lines are valid JSON with time, level, msg fields
steps.observed: All 11 lines are valid JSON. All have time, level, msg fields.

steps.action: curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:3100/health to generate request log
steps.observed: New log line with request_id appeared in log file

steps.action: Check request_id in request-context log lines
steps.observed: All 8 request-context lines have request_id (UUID format). 3 startup lines correctly omit request_id (not in request context).

steps.action: python3 JSON validation of entire log file
steps.expected: All lines parse as valid JSON
steps.observed: python3 json.loads() succeeded for all 11 lines (exit code 0)

frictions.description: go run does not properly forward SIGTERM to the child Go process, so graceful shutdown cannot be tested with go run. Must build binary and send SIGTERM directly to the Go process PID (identified via lsof).
frictions.resolution: Built binary with go build, started directly, identified Go process PID with lsof -i :3199, sent SIGTERM to that PID.

summary: Tested 3 assertions: 3 passed, 0 failed. Health endpoint returns 200 with {"status":"ok"}, service starts/stops cleanly with proper graceful shutdown logging, and all log output is valid structured JSON with time/level/msg fields and request_id in request context.
